quirk: Always listens to metal music before a match

name: Gustaf Petersen
bio: The tenacious Danish keeper with a love for baking and a mean save. His powerful physique and presence playing style make him a commanding figure in the box. When he's not saving shots, he's probably whipping up a cake.
quirk: Always brings a cake to the team's dressing room

name: Danilo Mendes
bio: The influential Brazilian defender with a love for samba music and a mean tackle. His robust physique and leader playing style make him a rock at the back. When he's not defending, he's probably dancing the samba.
quirk: Always wears a samba-inspired kit

name: Pieter Janssen
bio: The determined Dutch midfielder with a love for cycling and a mean dribble. His speedy physique and winger playing style make him a threat on the pitch. When he's not creating chances, he's probably riding his bike.
quirk: Always rides his bike to the stadium

name: Vince Briggs
bio: The tenacious English defender with a love for weightlifting and a mean tackle. His robust physique and enforcer playing style make him a force to be reckoned with. When he's not defending, he's probably pumping iron.
quirk: Always carries a dumbbell in his kit bag

name: Bruce Herrick
bio: The reliable English midfielder with a love for gardening and a mean pass. His dynamic physique and outlet playing style make him a solid addition to any team. When he's not creating chances, he's probably tending to his garden.
quirk: Always brings a packet of seeds to the stadium

name: Ishmael Grant
bio: The no-nonsense Jamaican defender with a love for reggae music and a mean tackle. His powerful physique and rock playing style make him a rock at the back. When he's not defending, he's probably jamming to reggae.
quirk: Always listens to reggae music before a match

name: Adolfo Pino
bio: The composed Italian forward with a love for opera and a mean finish. His nimble physique and trequartista playing style make him a threat on the pitch. When he's not scoring goals, he's probably singing an aria.
quirk: Always sings an opera song after scoring a goal

name: Enrico Gamarra
bio: The elegant Paraguayan forward with a love for tango music and a mean header. His powerful physique and target playing style make him a threat in the air. When he's not scoring goals, he's probably dancing the tango.
quirk: Always wears a tango-inspired kit

name: Abi Fatah
bio: Egypt's finest export since the pyramids, Abi Fatah is a wizard on the wing. His dribbling skills are so sharp, he's been known to leave defenders in the desert. When he's not playing football, he's solving Rubik's cubes blindfolded.
quirk: Only scores with his non-dominant foot on Fridays

name: Arvid Petersen
bio: Denmark's safest hands since the invention of the Lego brick, Arvid Petersen is a goalkeeper with a presence as commanding as a Viking war cry. He's also a part-time baker, famous for his 'goal-line' gateau.
quirk: Refuses to wear gloves on Sundays

name: Marky James
bio: England's answer to David Copperfield, Marky James makes defenders disappear with his magical footwork. When not conjuring goals out of thin air, he's a children's party entertainer, specializing in balloon animals.
quirk: Only celebrates goals with a magic trick

name: Matty Gibbs
bio: The fastest man in England, Matty Gibbs can leave defenders for dead and still have time for a Sunday roast. His secret training method? Chasing after his pet cheetah, Lightning.
quirk: Has a pet cheetah that runs alongside him during matches

name: Hugo Jamieson
bio: The rock at the back, Hugo Jamieson is as solid as the English countryside. When not defending, he's a keen Morris dancer, known for his impressive bell-shaking skills.
quirk: Only heads the ball while shouting 'Huzzah!'

name: Mitchell Todd
bio: The metronome in midfield, Mitchell Todd keeps the team ticking like a well-oiled clock. His other passion? Collecting antique timepieces, which he proudly displays in his 'time museum' at home.
quirk: Wears a different watch for every match

name: Hamish Horn
bio: Ireland's toughest defender since the invention of the Celtic cross, Hamish Horn is as robust as a pint of Guinness. He's also a master of the ancient art of Extreme Ironing.
quirk: Ironing his kit before every match

name: Chaz Field
bio: England's most reliable wingback since the invention of the queue, Chaz Field is as dependable as a London bus. He's also a part-time beekeeper, producing his own brand of 'Field's Fancy Honey'.
quirk: Keeps a beehive in his backyard

name: Orjan Oddmund
bio: Norway's most influential defender since the Viking era, Orjan Oddmund is as commanding as a fjord. He's also a secret knitting enthusiast, creating intricate scarves for his teammates.
quirk: Knits scarves for his teammates during away games

name: Bernard Oswald
bio: England's most composed midfielder since the invention of the stiff upper lip, Bernard Oswald is as calm as a cup of tea. He's also a master of the ancient art of Origami, creating intricate paper cranes before every match.
quirk: Folds origami cranes before every match

name: Isaac DePalma
bio: USA's most composed defender since the invention of the baseball mitt, Isaac DePalma is as solid as the Statue of Liberty. He's also a part-time DJ, spinning tracks under the alias 'DePalma the Destroyer'.
quirk: Spins tracks in the locker room before matches

name: Franklin Gorham
bio: England's most industrious midfielder since the invention of the factory whistle, Franklin Gorham is as tireless as a steam engine. He's also a secret line dancer, performing choreographed routines with his teammates.
quirk: Performs line dances in the tunnel before matches

name: Ted Sainsbury
bio: England's most reliable fullback since the invention of the queue, Ted Sainsbury is as dependable as a London bus. He's also a part-time baker, famous for his 'Sainsbury's Scones'.
quirk: Bakes scones for his teammates on away days

name: Maryan Lerond
bio: France's most composed midfielder since the invention of the beret, Maryan Lerond is as calm as a French café. He's also a master of the ancient art of Juggling, keeping three balls in the air during team talks.
quirk: Juggles three balls during team talks

name: Gianni Gelato
bio: Italy's most mercurial forward since the invention of the Vespa, Gianni Gelato is as unpredictable as a Italian traffic jam. He's also a part-time gelato maker, creating unique flavors for his teammates.
quirk: Creates a new gelato flavor for every goal he scores

name: Nick Guinness
bio: Scotland's most reliable defender since the invention of the kilt, Nick Guinness is as solid as a Scottish castle. He's also a secret bagpipe player, performing before every match to intimidate the opposition.
quirk: Plays the bagpipes before every match

name: Ken Terry
bio: Scotland's most charismatic midfielder since the invention of the tartan, Ken Terry is as charming as a Scottish folk song. He's also a part-time magician, performing tricks for his teammates on away days.
quirk: Performs magic tricks on the team bus

name: Duncan White
bio: England's most tenacious midfielder since the invention of the bulldog, Duncan White is as feisty as a English pub brawl. He's also a secret gardener, tending to his prize-winning roses during his free time.
quirk: Tends to his roses during halftime

name: Shane Rice
bio: England's most resolute forward since the invention of the fish and chips, Shane Rice is as solid as a English breakfast. He's also a part-time fisherman, reeling in the big ones on his days off.
quirk: Goes fishing on his days off

name: Gerald Sherwood
bio: England's most tenacious defender since the invention of the suit of armor, Gerald Sherwood is as robust as a medieval castle. He's also a secret medieval reenactor, participating in battles on the weekends.
quirk: Participates in medieval battles on the weekends

name: Javier Romero
bio: Spain's safest hands, Javier Romero has been known to lull opponents to sleep with his soothing goalkeeping melodies. His composed nature makes him a wall in the box. He's also a part-time flamenco dancer.
quirk: Refuses to wear gloves on Sundays

name: Cian Stone
bio: Cian Stone is the rock that England's defense is built upon. His influential presence in the backline is only rivalled by his impressive collection of antique teapots. He's a commander on the pitch and a connoisseur off it.
quirk: Only gives interviews over tea

name: Julien Amor
bio: Julien Amor is France's reliable fullback with a penchant for 80s disco music. His agile physique allows him to cover more ground than a Tour de France cyclist. He's the first to the party and the last to leave.
quirk: Wears disco-ball boots during matches

name: Andoni Nadal
bio: Andoni Nadal is the competitive heartbeat of Spain's midfield. His balanced physique and playmaking skills make him a nightmare to defend against. He's also a competitive eater, holding the record for most tapas consumed in one sitting.
quirk: Eats a whole pizza by himself after every win

name: Danny Kouakou
bio: Danny Kouakou is England's enforcer, a powerful defender with a reliable presence in the backline. His physique is only rivalled by his impressive collection of vintage cars. He's a stopper on the pitch and a speed demon off it.
quirk: Arrives at matches in a different vintage car each time

name: Rene Heisserer
bio: Rene Heisserer is France's wizard on the wing, a crafty dribbler with a penchant for magic tricks. His agile physique allows him to beat defenders with ease. He's a master of misdirection on and off the pitch.
quirk: Makes the ball disappear during matches

name: Syver Evensen
bio: Syver Evensen is Norway's towering defender, a composed presence in the backline. His aerial ability is only rivalled by his love of extreme ironing. He's a tower on the pitch and a thrill-seeker off it.
quirk: Ironing his kit during halftime

name: Drake Holden
bio: Drake Holden is Australia's libero goalkeeper, a composed and athletic presence between the posts. His distributing skills are only rivalled by his love of playing the didgeridoo. He's a commander on the pitch and a musician off it.
quirk: Plays the didgeridoo during warm-ups

name: Manuel Rizzo
bio: Manuel Rizzo is Argentina's commanding goalkeeper, an antagonistic presence between the posts. His athletic physique and distributing skills make him a nightmare to defend against. He's a commander on the pitch and a provocateur off it.
quirk: Argues with the referee in Spanish

name: Gift Ikechi
bio: Gift Ikechi is Nigeria's creative forward, a composed and athletic presence on the pitch. His trequartista playing style is only rivalled by his love of writing poetry. He's a creator on the pitch and a wordsmith off it.
quirk: Writes poems about his goals

name: Cenk Mehmet
bio: Cenk Mehmet is Turkey's regista midfielder, a competitive and stocky presence on the pitch. His controlling skills are only rivalled by his love of cooking Turkish delight. He's a controller on the pitch and a chef off it.
quirk: Brings Turkish delight to the dressing room

name: Diego Mesa
bio: Diego Mesa is Colombia's classy forward, a powerful and determined presence on the pitch. His hitman playing style is only rivalled by his love of salsa dancing. He's a striker on the pitch and a dancer off it.
quirk: Dances salsa after every goal

name: Herman Merton
bio: Herman Merton is England's reliable goalkeeper, a robust and composed presence between the posts. His wall-like defending is only rivalled by his love of gardening. He's a wall on the pitch and a green thumb off it.
quirk: Tends to his garden during halftime

name: Torsten Werner
bio: Torsten Werner is Germany's towering defender, a physical and imposing presence in the backline. His aerial ability is only rivalled by his love of heavy metal music. He's a tower on the pitch and a headbanger off it.
quirk: Headbangs to heavy metal during warm-ups

name: Sergio Vasquez
bio: Sergio Vasquez is Spain's deadly forward, a determined and pacy presence on the pitch. His rifle-like finishing is only rivalled by his love of playing the guitar. He's a striker on the pitch and a musician off it.
quirk: Plays the guitar after every match

name: Hayden Monday
bio: Hayden Monday is England's tenacious forward, a robust and energetic presence on the pitch. His bison-like playing style is only rivalled by his love of playing rugby. He's a bison on the pitch and a flanker off it.
quirk: Tackles opponents like a rugby player

name: Sergei Myslenko
bio: Sergei Myslenko is Russia's classy winger, a speedy and agile presence on the pitch. His wizard-like dribbling is only rivalled by his love of playing chess. He's a wizard on the pitch and a grandmaster off it.
quirk: Thinks three moves ahead like a chess player

name: Matt Winter
bio: Matt Winter is England's energetic defender, a reliable and athletic presence on the pitch. His bison-like playing style is only rivalled by his love of playing ice hockey. He's a bison on the pitch and a defenseman off it.
quirk: Hits opponents like a hockey player

name: Gary Pinbrick
bio: Gary Pinbrick is England's commanding goalkeeper, a reliable and athletic presence between the posts. His presence-like defending is only rivalled by his love of playing the harmonica. He's a commander on the pitch and a blues musician off it.
quirk: Plays the harmonica during warm-ups

name: Arthur Neele
bio: Arthur Neele is England's composed defender, a lynchpin-like presence in the backline. His stopper-like defending is only rivalled by his love of playing cricket. He's a stopper on the pitch and a bowler off it.
quirk: Bowls like a cricketer when taking throw-ins
